pages.raw_text:
The Press last week announced
that the North Coast road is in
reality a branch of the Chicago
Northwestern, in connection with
which the following railroad in
formation given from a reliable
source in Tacoma is of especial in.
terest.


The Tacoma report says that the
Chicago & Northwestern railway
has wrested the control of the
Northern Pacific from Jim Hill and
is coming to Tacoma.


For the last two years the state
ment has been persisted in that
the Northwestern was coming to
Tacoma and that it would be here
as soon as the Milwaukee, its
great rival. There has been much
speculation as to how it proposed
to- get here.


From the statements now being
discussed among the knowing
ones in railroad circles it appears
it does not have to build a new
line, but that 1t has gobbled the
N. p, with the help of the Nor
gan interests in the East, and is
going to land here over the N. P.
lines.


The situation probably explains
why Jim Hill fastened, while he
still had control Of the N. p, to
buy from it tidelands for his Great
Northern road for terminals in Ia
coma.


- AS the story comes now, the die
has been cast in the railroad situa-
ton and Hill has been dethroned
from his position of dominance in
the N. P. 1t is said Harry San
born, son of S. Sanborn, the for
mer general manager of the whole
Northwestern system, is coming
to Tacoma soon as general west
ern manager and that Manager H.
, c. Mutt will retire.


Information has been secured
indicating that the deal is a GER
tainty and that the Northwestern
will, before many moons, be in
full control of the N. P. and will
be putting her trains into
Tacoma.


Pres. Strahorn, of the North
Coast, was reported in Tacoma
last week negotiating for the sites
of the st. Paul mill, Wheeler &
Osgood's big factory and the Car
stems packing plant for railroad
terminals,
